THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: THIS COURT NOW ENTERS JUDGMENT IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF SAFEWAY INC. AND AGAINST DEFENDANT AZIZA GRAVES ON THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TRESPASS AND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NUISANCE. NOMINAL DAMAGES OF $1.00 ARE AWARDED TO SAFEWAY INC. AND AGAINST DEFENDANT AZIZA GRAVES ON EACH OF THE TWO CAUSES OF ACTION. AZIZA GRAVES AND HER RESPECTIVE AGENTS, ASSOCIATES, SOLICITORS, SERVANTS, VOLUNTEERS, EMPLOYEES, ASSIGNS AND ALL THOSE ACTING IN CONCERT WITH HER OR AT HER REQUEST OR INSTRUCTION OR FOR HER ARE PERMANENTLY RESTRAINED, PROHIBITED AND ENJOINED FROM ENTERING OR BEING PRESENT AT ANY SAFEWAY STORE PREMISES IN CALIFORNIA, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO ALL PRIVATELY OWNED WALKWAYS DIRECTLY IN FRONT OF THE SAFEWAY STORE BUILDINGS, ALL STORE ENTRANCES, ENTRANCE CHANNELS, CURB CUTS, FIRE LANES AND EXIT RAMPS, AND ALL ADJOINING PRIVATELY-OWNED PARKING LOTS. (SEE SCANNED "JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT AZIZA GRAVES")

John Dahlberg, Esq., DILLINGHAM & MURPHY, LLP, (415) 397-2700, jnd@dillinhammurphy.com,
Attorneys for Petitioner SAFEWAY INC for Plaintiff.
No appearance by Defendant.

Court's Tenative Ruling is "Hearing Required" Defendant did not appear for today's hearing.

Hearing is held. NOT ARGUED.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE for Preliminary Injunction is granted. Plaintiff is required to pay undertaking in the amount of $1000.00 to the court. Plaintiff is to submit a revised proposed order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is ordered off calendar. This court's November 4, 2022 order was required to "be served by no later than 11/15/22," but no proof of service is found in the register of actions.
